kept_content: CVE-2024-10092 describes a critical remote code execution vulnerability in Apache Tomcat's default connector. Exploitation requires unauthenticated access to the management endpoint. Patch immediately.
XSS mitigation in modern SPAs requires strict output encoding and CSP headers. Frameworks like React auto-escape by default, but dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses this protection.
SQL injection prevention requires parameterized queries, not string concatenation. ORMs like SQLAlchemy handle this automatically, but raw queries remain dangerous.

Log4j (CVE-2021-44228) remains relevant. Many legacy systems still run vulnerable versions. Attack vectors include LDAP injection via log formatting. Patching requires JVM argument updates and library replacement.
Rate limiting and token bucket algorithms are essential for API defense. Implement at the gateway level using tools like Envoy or Kong to prevent abuse and DDoS amplification.
Cloud security posture management (CSPM) tools scan AWS/GCP/Azure for misconfigurations. Common findings: public S3 buckets, overly permissive IAM roles, unencrypted RDS instances.
Privilege escalation via sudo misconfigurations allows low-priv users to gain root access. Check /etc/sudoers for NOPASSWD rules and wildcard command permissions. Use visudo to edit safely.

analysis_guide.prompt_tuning: Adjust criteria in config.yaml prompt_template if needed
analysis_guide.cost_optimization: Increase chunk_length or enable regex pre-filter to reduce LLM calls
